Patch Set 9: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 9:

Hmm, if this patch is to split the server and client code and push to kernel tree. Do we really need push obdecho client to kernel tree, since mostly, echo client is mostly for performance tuning.

Patch Set 9:

I suggest that we don't bother with upstreaming echo client.

Patch Set 9: Looks good to me, approved

The client code is _already_ submitted upstream and includes the echo client. I think it does make sense to include the echo client in the upstream kernel for a number of reasons. This ensures that the code continues to be maintained and can run with that client as needed. It is also important for performance tuning with the vanilla kernel client. Finally, this will allow us to use the vanilla kernel client with our testing scripts.

Patch Set 9: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(5 inline comments)

I don't think we need to ifdef out tools changes.
Also I am not entirely sure it's a great idea to remove definitions of various constants, even if they are unused?

Patch Set 9:

I'm ok to keep the constants that are only used by server. They might be cleaned up in upstream kernel though because Greg KH might think differently. When that happens, we'll need to backport such patches to master.
Uploaded patch set 10.

Patch Set 10: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

I thought we decided not to push obdecho to linux kernel source? i.e. not to bother to split obdecho here? sorry to give - until this is clear.

Patch Set 10:

See Andreas' previous comments:

The client code is _already_ submitted upstream and includes the echo client. I think it does make sense to include the echo client in the upstream kernel for a number of reasons. This ensures that the code continues to be maintained and can run with that client as needed. It is also important for performance tuning with the vanilla kernel client. Finally, this will allow us to use the vanilla kernel client with our testing scripts.
Patch Set 10: Rebased

parents.subject: LU-4106 mdt: avoid recursive lu_object_find an object

commit.subject: LU-1330 obdecho: splits metadata echo client from client-side
commit.message:
LU-1330 obdecho: splits metadata echo client from client-side

Client-side module needs not to support MDS echo client. Comment out
(using HAVE_SERVER_SUPPORT) it to facilitate obdclass' split/cleanup.

Signed-off-by: Liu Xuezhao <xuezhao.liu@emc.com>
Signed-off-by: Peng Tao <tao.peng@emc.com>
Change-Id: Ie70d9a9bd665878d4ce7327c288fb55abd60c878
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/2674
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
